summary: Credential key lifecycle management implemented as a faithful mirror of the existing Fernet key sub-reconciler. Adds CredentialKeysSpec CRD type, reconcile_credential.go 5-step sub-reconciler (Secret → RBAC → CronJob → PushSecret → Condition), controller integration, [credential] config section, credential-keys hash annotation for rolling restarts, and comprehensive unit + E2E tests. The credentialRotateScript correctly includes both credential_rotate and credential_migrate commands. DRY refactoring of fernetKeysHash into a shared keysHash helper avoids code duplication. All 12 SHALL requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-012) are addressed.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests exist for reconcile_credential.go (11 test cases)
Webhook tests exist for CredentialKeys validation (maxActiveKeys, cron)
Deployment tests exist for credentialKeysHash and annotation
Controller tests updated with testCredentialKeysSecret for full reconciliation
E2E Chainsaw test covers rotation lifecycle
Edge cases covered: minActiveKeys floor, empty hash, secret not found

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing Fernet 5-step sub-reconciler pattern exactly
DRY: keysHash() shared function eliminates duplication
No dead code or unused imports
go vet passes with zero errors

security_checklist.item: RBAC uses minimal permissions (get+patch on specific Secret only)
No hardcoded secrets — keys generated via crypto/rand
TLS verification in rotation script (ssl.create_default_context with CA)
No secrets passed as CLI arguments — SA token read from file
credential_migrate ensures encrypted credentials remain accessible after rotation

architecture_checklist.item: Solution mirrors Fernet pattern — minimal conceptual overhead
CredentialKeysReady condition inserted between FernetKeysReady and DatabaseReady
credential-keys volume Optional flag removed (Secret guaranteed to exist)
CRD manifests synced between config/crd/bases and helm/crds
make generate && make manifests && make sync-crds produce no uncommitted diffs

dry_yagni_checklist.item: keysHash() shared function avoids duplicating fernetKeysHash logic
generateFernetKey() reused for credential keys (same format)
No premature abstractions — credential/fernet sub-reconcilers kept separate by design

fail_fast_checklist.item: Secret creation requeues immediately to confirm availability
Error wrapping preserves IsNotFound via %w for caller error handling
Defense-in-depth validation for MaxActiveKeys < 3 in webhook

defensive_checklist.item: max() floor ensures at least 3 keys even if spec bypasses webhook
Cron expression validated in webhook for credential keys

issues_found.description: E2E test script uses 'set -eu' without 'pipefail'. The script contains pipe chains (kubectl ... | sha256sum | cut) where a failure in kubectl could be silently swallowed by a succeeding cut command. The Chainsaw E2E test pattern documentation recommends 'set -euo pipefail' for multi-command scripts. However, this is consistent with the existing fernet-rotation E2E test which also uses 'set -eu'.
issues_found.fix: Change 'set -eu' to 'set -euo pipefail' to catch pipeline failures. Apply the same fix to the fernet-rotation test for consistency.

suggested_improvements: Consider adding 'set -euo pipefail' to both credential-rotation and fernet-rotation E2E test scripts for pipeline failure safety (both currently use 'set -eu').

detected_patterns.name: Shared keysHash helper with suffix-based Secret lookup
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding a new key type (beyond Fernet and credential) that requires a Secret hash for Deployment annotation-based rolling restarts
detected_patterns.description: When multiple key types (Fernet, credential) need deterministic SHA-256 hashing of their Secret data for Deployment pod-template annotations, a shared keysHash(ctx, keystone, suffix) function accepts a suffix parameter to construct the Secret name as '{name}-{suffix}'. Type-specific wrappers (fernetKeysHash, credentialKeysHash) delegate to the shared function. This avoids duplicating the Get+Marshal+Hash logic while keeping type-specific callers readable.
